q:
The dipped headlights are switched on.
What is your reaction.

A. You just drive on.
B. You give priority.
e:
LESSON 17 – ‘Qualified persons’.
Only when the blue flashing lights burn and you hear the alarm signal, you should give priority.

q:
When a police officer whistles, the drivers must stop.

A. Correct.
B. Incorrect.
e:
LESSON 17 – ‘Qualified persons’.
If he blows on a whistle, this means that you need to look to him and to the command he gives or is going to give.

q:
A tram driver doesn’t need to take into account the orders of an authorized person.

A. Correct.
B. Incorrect.
e:
LESSON 17 – ‘Qualified persons’.
Tram drivers do not have to follow the traffic regulations, except the orders of an authorized person and the traffic lights.

LESSON 17 – ‘Qualified persons’.

Who must stop?

LESSON 17 – ‘Qualified persons’.
When an officer raises one arm vertically, all road users, including pedestrians, must stop.

q:
You are entering an intersection. Suddenly, a police officer raises his arm.
What do you do?

A. You stop.
B. You stop and drive backwards.
C. You leave the intersection.
e:
LESSON 17
When an officer raises his arm, it means that all vehicles must stop.
Vehicles already in the intersection must leave it as quickly as possible, continuing in the direction they intended to go.
clean_q:
You are entering an intersection. Suddenly, a police officer raises his arm.
What do you do?
A. You stop.
B. You stop and drive backwards.
C. You leave the intersection.
clean_e:
LESSON 17
When an officer raises his arm, it means that all vehicles must stop.
Vehicles already in the intersection must leave it as quickly as possible, continuing in the direction they intended to go.

q:
You are approaching an intersection where an officer is standing with one arm extended horizontally.
What instruction is the officer giving?

A. You must turn left.
B. You may continue driving, but not turn left.
C. You must stop.
e:
LESSON 17
When an officer extends one or both arms horizontally, road users approaching the officer’s chest or back must stop.
Road users approaching the officer’s fingertips or side may continue onto the intersection to go straight, turn left, or turn right.
An officer with both arms extended horizontally gives exactly the same instruction as with only one arm extended horizontally.
clean_q:
You are approaching an intersection where an officer is standing with one arm extended horizontally.

B. You may continue driving, but not turn left.
C. You must stop.
clean_e:
LESSON 17
When an officer extends one or both arms horizontally, road users approaching the officer’s chest or back must stop.
Road users approaching the officer’s fingertips or side may continue onto the intersection to go straight, turn left, or turn right.
An officer with both arms extended horizontally gives exactly the same instruction as with only one arm extended horizontally.

q:
What is the hierarchical order?

A. 1 - 2 - 3.
B. 3 - 2 - 1.
C. 3 - 1 - 2.
e:
LESSON 17 – ‘Qualified persons’.
At the top of the commands of an agent.
Then the traffic lights.
Than the road signs.
Then the traffic rules.

q:
What must drivers do who are on the road that is blocked by the police officer’s horizontally extended arm?

A. Stop.
B. Turn right.
e:
LESSON 17
When a police officer extends one or both arms horizontally, all drivers approaching the officer’s front or back must stop.
➡️ Continuing to drive or turning, as this driver intends to do, is a fourth-degree offence.

q:
Traffic sergeants, group leaders for cyclists or motorcyclists on a junction with traffic lights have the same authorization as an authorized person.

A. Correct.
B. Incorrect.
e:
LESSON 17 – ‘Qualified persons’.
They may only give directions and have no authorization.
At a crossroads with traffic lights, they can do nothing.

q:
If a police officer raises his arm, must a tram also stop?

A. Yes.
B. No, because a tram always has priority.
e:
LESSON 17
When a police officer raises his arm, all drivers must stop.
Drivers who are already on the intersection must leave it as quickly as possible.
A tram normally has priority, but it must always obey the instructions of an authorised person and traffic lights.

LESSON 17

Drivers who are already on the intersection must leave it as quickly as possible.
A tram normally has priority, but it must always obey the instructions of an authorised person and traffic lights.

LESSON 17
When a police officer raises one arm vertically at an intersection, all road users must stop.
Anyone already on the intersection must leave it as quickly as possible.
